title: The two-point trapezoidal rule is nonnegative on nonnegative data

source.citation: Alfio Quarteroni, Riccardo Sacco, and Fausto Saleri, Numerical Mathematics, 2nd ed., Springer, 2007, Ch. 9 (Numerical Integration).
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If the cell width and endpoint samples are nonnegative, the one-cell trapezoidal-rule value is nonnegative.
informal.expected_conclusion: The trapezoidal rule returns a nonnegative value.

declarations.name: trapezoid2
declarations.statement: def trapezoid2 (h f0 f1 : Real) : Real := h * (f0 + f1) / 2

declarations.name: trapezoid2_nonneg
declarations.statement: theorem trapezoid2_nonneg (h f0 f1 : Real) (hh : 0 <= h) (hf0 : 0 <= f0) (hf1 : 0 <= f1) : 0 <= trapezoid2 h f0 f1

tasks.input: Translate the quadrature theorem `trapezoid2_nonneg` into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem trapezoid2_nonneg (h f0 f1 : Real) (hh : 0 <= h) (hf0 : 0 <= f0) (hf1 : 0 <= f1) : 0 <= trapezoid2 h f0 f1
unfold trapezoid2; nlinarith [mul_nonneg hh (add_nonneg hf0 hf1)]

tasks.input: Identify the main algebraic or order lemma needed for the quadrature proof.
